content:
#!/usr/bin/env python # Copyright Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. # SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT """Creates an HTML page for releases for `pip install --find-links` from a subdirectory in an S3 bucket. For generating an index from local files (e.g. during a CI build), see generate_local_index.py. Sample usage: ```bash ./build_tools/packaging/python/generate_s3_index.py \ --bucket=therock-dev-python \ --endpoint=s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com \ --subdir=gfx110X-all \ --output=index.html ``` S3 buckets in `us-east-2` currently used are: * `therock-dev-python` * `therock-nightly-python` """ import argparse import boto3 import html import io import sys import textwrap from urllib.parse import quote def parse_arguments(): p = argparse.ArgumentParser() p.add_argument( "--bucket", required=True, help="S3 bucket in which the subdirectory is indexed", ) p.add_argument( "--endpoint", default="s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com", help="S3 endpoint used to form the URL", ) p.add_argument( "--subdir", "--subdirectory", required=True, help="Subdirectory in S3 bucket which is indexed", ) p.add_argument( "--output", default="-", help="The file to write the HTML to or '-' for stdout (the default)", ) return p.parse_args() def get_objects(bucket_name: str, subdir: str): s3 = boto3.resource("s3") bucket = s3.Bucket(bucket_name) all_objects = bucket.objects.filter(Prefix=subdir) objects = [obj.key.split(subdir + "/")[1] for obj in all_objects] if "index.html" in objects: objects.remove("index.html") return objects def add_releases(objects: list, base_url: str, subdir: str, file: io.TextIOWrapper): file.write( f'

ROCm/TheRock {subdir} packages

\n' ) for obj in objects: encoded_url = f"{base_url}/{quote(obj)}" # URL-encode the object key name = html.escape(obj) file.write(f' {name}
\n') file.write( "
\n" f' This file was auto-generated with generate_s3_index.py.\n' ) def main(args): objects = get_objects(args.bucket, args.subdir) url = f"https://{args.bucket}.{args.endpoint}/{args.subdir}" with sys.stdout if args.output == "-" else open(args.output, "w") as f: f.write( textwrap.dedent( """\ """ ) ) add_releases(objects, url, args.subdir, f) f.write( textwrap.dedent( """\ """ ) ) if __name__ == "__main__": main(parse_arguments())
